clusters.title: Document Import Adapters and Clipboard Normalization Trade-offs
clusters.summary: Discusses using MarkItDown for file imports (PDF/DOCX) versus separate clipboard HTML cleanup, benchmarking adapter fidelity and cost, and the distinction between import adapters and live clipboard processing.
keyPoints: MarkItDown is for file imports, not the live clipboard hot path.
Clipboard rich-text cleanup is a separate normalization problem.
Need artifact-level benchmarks to compare import adapters on fidelity and cost.
HTML cleanup for copied rich text remains distinct from file conversion.
clusters.reason: Captures focus on document-to-Markdown conversion tools, their appropriate use cases, and benchmarking, aligning closely with the existing 'Document Markdown Normalization' topic.

possibleTopics.reason: Directly discusses MarkItDown, import adapters, and normalization trade-offs.

clusters.title: Runtime Contract and Isolation for Import Adapters
clusters.summary: Notes that import adapters should run inside Rust background jobs, with Python-based adapters acceptable if isolated behind async task boundaries, maintaining the runtime contract in the Rust compiler layer.
keyPoints: Import adapters should run inside Rust background jobs, not renderer hooks.
Scheduling and retry semantics belong to the compiler runtime.
Python-based adapters are acceptable if isolated behind async task boundaries.
The runtime contract belongs to the Rust compiler layer.
clusters.reason: Captures discuss where import adapter logic should execute (Rust background jobs) and the runtime contract, fitting the 'Rust Background Compiler' topic's scope.

possibleTopics.reason: Focuses on runtime execution, scheduling, and task boundaries for background jobs.

clusters.title: Priority for Clipboard Eval Over Document Imports
clusters.summary: A Slack message stating to ship clipboard evaluation first and document imports second, to avoid derailing the quality pipeline milestone.
keyPoints: Ship clipboard eval first, document imports second.
The import path should not derail the quality pipeline milestone.
clusters.reason: This is a single, tactical message about project priorities and milestone sequencing. It doesn't form a full knowledge cluster and is better suited for inbox review to inform planning.

possibleTopics.reason: Mentions 'clipboard eval' and 'quality pipeline', which relates to evaluation workstreams.

missingContext: What specific 'clipboard eval' and 'quality pipeline milestone' this refers to.
